item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
The image originates form here, which is protected under a copyright. As such, it is not compliant with CC 3.0 and is pending staff decision for removal/replacement.

Edit: image has since been removed

item.title: Re: +500
item.content:encoded: Oh that's good too. It's in an interest-accruing account, right? Because I've got like 8 years worth of upvotes I'm owed. By my estimate, compounding annually and assuming a fair market rate of return, I should be receiving around $4.3 billion (after taxes).

item.title: Re: +500
item.content:encoded: It's actually a check, believe it or not. Conversion rate varies but right now it's about 5 upvotes per dollar USD.

It's probably lame to be following my own SCP and desperately waiting for it to break +500, but dammit I'm proud all the same.

So is my trophy going to go come in the mail, or do I need to, like, go pick it up or something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: damn, how do you people come up with this shit? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I completely missed the interview link my first time through here.
It adds so much.

item.title: Image Update
item.content:encoded:
Edited the original image (still attached) to look more like real security footage
(added sharpness, noise, fisheye distortion, desaturation, etc.)

Updated with permission from the author, DrSevere

item.title: Re: Someone is making a game of this
item.content:encoded:
Holy crap, this is coolest thing ever. Someone actually went and made a game of something I wrote.

I want that on my tombstone.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Staff only deletes comments that contain severe rule-breaking material, like links to virus sites or trolling. You're fine here.

Just a heads up though, sometimes people do post info about new SCP-based games in the Fan Work forum. There's a bit more visibility for this sort of discussion in that location. :)

item.title: Someone is making a game of this
item.content:encoded:
Someone is making a game of this. It's working but short. I think it will be fleshed out a bit more:
http://gamejolt.com/games/scp-022/140344

I'm not the developer (or even know him/her) I just thought it was worth putting here. If it's not ok to post this, I apologize and will delete this comment (if a mod doesn't delete it).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, it's this photo, but posterized.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this "image" is clearly a drawing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't particularly care for it at first, but the fact that the bodies appeared to be those stolen from other morgues kept me reading, and the interviews really sold me on it.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like this one a lot. It's one that is creepy, weird, makes you unsure what is going on, and has some strange occurrences that can be tested and observed more your average, "strange stuff happens in this building." People being put inside come back as corpses, the corpses aren't in their right bodies, what the what?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh the Twilight Zone, my favorite
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember reading this before I joined. Can't believe I haven't upvoted until now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interview sold me on this one. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is excellent. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the main article, particularly the first few paragraphs are a little wordy, but that's just a minor thing, and it didn't detract too much from the article. Other than that I liked it, and the interview log made it even better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually have to disagree, I think the Interview Log is really what makes this one work. The idea of getting stuck in another's dead body is where the creep really comes out and I think it's an essential aspect of this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interview log really makes this one. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please type normal in the forums -Bright

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Always liked this one - am surprised to find out it's fairly new. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, good catch with the typo. 41 votes and you are the first person to spot it.

Anyway, in response to your questions, the idea is that there is an area outside the morgue, like the room just outside it, which contains the incineration equipment and such. It isn't until an instance leaves the morgue itself that it gets lit up and burned. The vault is locking the entrance to that second, non-special room, if that makes sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First, I love it, it's got a great classic vibe to it. Great idea and great execution. It was an easy upvote. That said, I'm left wanting for clarity in the second part of the containment procedure.

"In the event than an instance of SCP-022-1 appears, automated systems should incinerate it the moment it leaves SCP-022."

Do you mean "that" instead of "than?" I would fix the typo myself but I honestly don't know which you want. You seem to want to preserve instances of SCP-022-1 for interviews, so this might be something other than SCP-022-1 that the containment team neutralizes on sight.

"At this point the vault door may be unlocked to admit cleanup crews."

How do they clean up wthout entering?

"Should the automated systems fail to destroy the instance of SCP-022-1, response teams are cleared to enter and neutralize it."

Wouldn't they disappear?

Maybe I'm being thick, but it was hard to understand the containment procedures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha, sorry, I'm in four engineering classes right now and every one insists on doing imperial units as often (or more often) than metric units, so my hatred levels for the imperial system are pretty high right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Take a chill pill Severe. It's just Imperial.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're far too intense about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's one of the big differences between metric and US units (besides the fact that US are just fucking STUPID). In the metric, they talk about how much of something there is with mass, which is constant no matter where you go in the universe. In the US system, they use weight, however, which is a measurement of force. God I hate my country sometimes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Newtons are fine, hardly 'esoteric', but it's not a direct equivalent for 'weighing' a kilogram. 1 N = 1 kg undergoing acceleration of 1 ms-2. Therefore a mass of 1 kg will weigh ~10 N (accel. due to gravity is 9.81 ms-2).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really? It's just the proper way of saying something 'weighs a kilogram.' Never knew it was an esoteric unit.

item.title: What others have said
item.content:encoded: What can I say… It works. An intriguing and somewhat disturbing concept, very good execution… Excellent job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, I missed that. Guess I'm not as perceptive as I like to purport.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not entirely sure what you mean. I included the bit about them gaining an additional 500 N of lifting force, though I also gave that in pounds since newtons seem to be the one metric measurement no one has any reference point for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. It's classic, but in a totally positive way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, you took out the 150 Newtons thing. Too bad this had to replace mine. ;~;

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really good.

*kerchunk* "Must…get…out…" twitch. twitch. twitch. "…dang." *kerchunk* *sizzle*

As far as I can tell it's not too cliched or overdone and it's certainly not on the big list. Definitely one of the best ideas in here for a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No. A reference to 008 would detract from the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good writing, good concept, good horror, you don't need to read the supplements for it to be good… Solid stuff, all around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interview log really made this one memorable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha I figured. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No insult meant. I was just saying that having dead people wake up in different bodies was unexpected and cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A lot of people have compared this to 008, and I am considering just mentioning it in the article (with senior staff permission). It would most likely be something to the effect of "Analysis of tissue reveals no traces of SCP-008" or something along those lines.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's kind of fun to think about the reactions of the first team from the Foundation to find this thing. They had to be about ready to nuke the place, fearing an outbreak of SCP-008…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You certainly emulated the classic style. This is really good for… well, for what it is. I'm impressed with how well this turned out.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! Going into this I knew it would be a bit cliche, but I really wanted this to be sort of a classic style, like 087 (though not nearly as scary, obviously). I'm glad it turned out well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, this hits a couple Cliches (Even though they might not be in the big list), but it really does it well. It's nothing huge that takes a lot to explain, it's just a reanimating morgue! I do like how one person can be put into another's body… The interview log enhances it for sure, it's not overdone at all. Like the others said, it hits all the right spots. You have my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …I feel like that is an insult/compliment hybrid =P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. I wouldn't call it the most novel concept ever, and if I saw someone proposing it in chat, I'd have probably said to not even bother writing it, but…

It just… works. It's interesting, creepy, and mysterious - the three key ingredients of a good SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one. It's simple, its spooky, and the interview log was far better than I was expecting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So yeah, with this one I kind of wanted to go for a classic horror vibe, since, I mean, it's a goddamn morgue. Corpses are creepy.

Just in case you missed it, there is an Interview Log as well. As always, all constructive feedback is appreciated!
